title: VPI tests failing to run with Icarus
body:
Using the current development version (commit 0b23787d6c495a3dc703708c3574b741bcfa9ac0) of Verilator on the master branch within the provided devcontainer as well as the current development version (commit d67d3323ad7da3aa2e6b1afb6da7be313c4848a6) of Icarus, I am attempting to run
`test_regress/t/t_vpi_var.py --iv`
and
`test_regress/t/t_vpi_get_value_array.py --iv`

However, these tests fail to run. The output for `test_regress/t/t_vpi_var.py --iv` is

```
driver.py: Found 22 cores, using -j 22
driver.py: Found 22 cores, using -j 22
==SUMMARY: Left 1  Passed 0  Failed 0  Time 0:00
======================================================================
iv/t_vpi_var: ==================================================
        iverilog -D IVERILOG -g2012 -oobj_iv/t_vpi_var/simiv -g2005-sv -D USE_VPI_NOT_DPI -DWAVES -f input.vc -D TEST_OBJ_DIR=obj_iv/t_vpi_var -D TEST_DUMPFILE=obj_iv/t_vpi_var/simx.vcd -D USE_VPI_NOT_DPI t/t_vpi_var.v     > obj_iv/t_vpi_var/iv_compile.log
(null):2: Ignoring +librescan
(null):2: Ignoring +notimingchecks
t/t_vpi_var.v:62: internal error: I don't know how to elaborate(ivl_type_t) this expression: {'sd0, 'sd0, 'sd0, 'sd432}
Elaboration failed
%Warning: iv/t_vpi_var: Exec of iverilog failed: t/t_vpi_var.v:62: internal error: I don't know how to elaborate(ivl_type_t) this expression: {'sd0, 'sd0, 'sd0, 'sd432}
iv/t_vpi_var: %Error: Exec of iverilog failed: t/t_vpi_var.v:62: internal error: I don't know how to elaborate(ivl_type_t) this expression: {'sd0, 'sd0, 'sd0, 'sd432}
iv/t_vpi_var: FAILED: Exec of iverilog failed: t/t_vpi_var.v:62: internal error: I don't know how to elaborate(ivl_type_t) this expression: {'sd0, 'sd0, 'sd0, 'sd432}
==SUMMARY: Passed 0  Failed 1  Time 0:00

======================================================================
#iv/t_vpi_var: %Error: Exec of iverilog failed: t/t_vpi_var.v:62: internal error: I don't know how to elaborate(ivl_type_t) this expression: {'sd0, 'sd0, 'sd0, 'sd432}
                make && test_regress/t/t_vpi_var.py  --iv
==TESTS DONE, FAILED: Passed 0  Failed 1  Time 0:00
```
And the output for `test_regress/t/t_vpi_get_value_array.py --iv` is
```
driver.py: Found 22 cores, using -j 22
driver.py: Found 22 cores, using -j 22
==SUMMARY: Left 1  Passed 0  Failed 0  Time 0:00
======================================================================
iv/t_vpi_get_value_array: ==================================================
        iverilog -D IVERILOG -g2012 -oobj_iv/t_vpi_get_value_array/simiv -g2005-sv -D USE_VPI_NOT_DPI -f input.vc -D TEST_OBJ_DIR=obj_iv/t_vpi_get_value_array -D TEST_DUMPFILE=obj_iv/t_vpi_get_value_array/simx.vcd -D USE_VPI_NOT_DPI -D VERILATOR_COMMENTS t/t_vpi_get_value_array.v     > obj_iv/t_vpi_get_value_array/iv_compile.log
(null):2: Ignoring +librescan
(null):2: Ignoring +notimingchecks
t/t_vpi_get_value_array.v:78: warning: Numeric constant truncated to 69 bits.
t/t_vpi_get_value_array.v:79: warning: Numeric constant truncated to 69 bits.
t/t_vpi_get_value_array.v:83: warning: Numeric constant truncated to 69 bits.
t/t_vpi_get_value_array.v:84: warning: Numeric constant truncated to 69 bits.
        c++ -I/workspaces/verilator/test_regress/../include/vltstd -I/workspaces/verilator/test_regress/../include -fPIC -shared -rdynamic -o obj_iv/t_vpi_get_value_array/libvpi.so -DIVERILOG -DIS_VPI  t/t_vpi_get_value_array.cpp   > obj_iv/t_vpi_get_value_array/pli_compile.log
        obj_iv/t_vpi_get_value_array/simiv   vvp -n -m obj_iv/t_vpi_get_value_array/libvpi.so   > obj_iv/t_vpi_get_value_array/vlt_sim.log
t/t_vpi_get_value_array.v:90: Error: System task/function $mon_check() is not defined by any module.
obj_iv/t_vpi_get_value_array/simiv: Program not runnable, 1 errors.
%Warning: iv/t_vpi_get_value_array: Exec of obj_iv/t_vpi_get_value_array/simiv failed: t/t_vpi_get_value_array.v:90: Error: System task/function $mon_check() is not defined by any module.
iv/t_vpi_get_value_array: %Error: Exec of obj_iv/t_vpi_get_value_array/simiv failed: t/t_vpi_get_value_array.v:90: Error: System task/function $mon_check() is not defined by any module.
iv/t_vpi_get_value_array: FAILED: Exec of obj_iv/t_vpi_get_value_array/simiv failed: t/t_vpi_get_value_array.v:90: Error: System task/function $mon_check() is not defined by any module.
==SUMMARY: Passed 0  Failed 1  Time 0:01

======================================================================
#iv/t_vpi_get_value_array: %Error: Exec of obj_iv/t_vpi_get_value_array/simiv failed: t/t_vpi_get_value_array.v:90: Error: System task/function $mon_check() is not defined by any module.
                make && test_regress/t/t_vpi_get_value_array.py  --iv
==TESTS DONE, FAILED: Passed 0  Failed 1  Time 0:01
```
In general, a lot of the vpi tests fail with Icarus, while some succeed. Here is a table of the results of running all the `test_regress/t/t_vpi*.py --iv` tests:

| Test Name  | Test Outcome |
| ------------- | ------------- |
| t_vpi_cb_iter.py | Passes |
| t_vpi_const_type.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_dump.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_dump_missing_scopes.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_dump_no_inline.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_escape.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_finish.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_get.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_get_public_rw_switch.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_get_value_array.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_memory.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_module.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_module_dpi.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_module_empty.py | Passes |
| t_vpi_multidim.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_onetime_cbs.py | Passes |
| t_vpi_package.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_param.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_public_depth.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_public_depth_off.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_public_depthn_1.py | Passes |
| t_vpi_public_depthn_2.py | Passes |
| t_vpi_public_depthn_3.py | Passes |
| t_vpi_public_off.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_public_params.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_put_value_array.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_release_dup_bad.py | Passes |
| t_vpi_repetitive_cbs.py | Passes |
| t_vpi_sc.py | Passes |
| t_vpi_stop_bad.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_time_cb.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_unimpl.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_var.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_var2.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_var3.py | Fails |
| t_vpi_zero_time_cb.py | Passes |

I would greatly appreciate any pointers as to whether I need to change something about my Icarus installation, or if the tests need to be updated.

Best regards
